meal_timing_tips.tip: Have your early morning drink/item within an hour of getting up.
Remember, if you skip breakfast you will likely eat more calories extra over the rest of the day. Ideal time: do not have breakfast later than 10am.
Have your mid-morning snack between breakfast and lunch. Ideal time: 11am-12pm.
Ideal lunch time: 12:30-2:30pm. Don't have lunch later than 4pm.
Have your mid-evening snack between lunch and dinner. Ideal time: 4-6pm.
Ideal dinner time: 7-9pm. Don't have dinner later than 10pm.
Drink 3 litres of water per day.
Exercise: daily walk for 40-45 minutes (brisk walk), or 'moon charged walking' on weeks where specified.

preparation_tips.tip: Smoothies should be consumed within 30 minutes of preparation.
No fermentation is required for instant dosa batters (e.g. green gram dosa) - can be made fresh after soaking and blending.
Don't add fruits if you are adding normal milk to a chia pudding or similar preparation (food combining guidance).
Most curries can be made as you would at home; just use less oil, and avoid pre-made spice podis/powders such as peanut podi or dry coconut podi.
If short on time to make roti for lunch, substitute a cheela made with the same flour combination plus added vegetables.
Jowar and Ragi flours should be shaped using the 'patting' method (pat the dough by hand on a cloth or butter paper) rather than rolled, since they don't hold together well when rolled.
Recipes can be adapted and altered as per availability and convenience.

posture_and_digestion_tips.tip: Vajrasan (a seated yoga posture) for at least 10 minutes should be done right after a meal, as it is known to facilitate nutrient absorption and aids in proper digestion. Avoid this posture if there is any leg or knee injury.

Before starting a meal, chew a one-inch piece of fresh ginger with a few drops of lemon juice and a pinch of salt. This activates salivary glands, producing the necessary enzymes for the body to more easily absorb nutrients from food.
Eat a small plate of salad first, before starting the main meal.
Turmeric tea after dinner (when prescribed in the plan) should not be skipped.

ingredient_specific_tips.tip: Skip ash gourd juice if prone to colds; alternatively, balance its coolness by adding pepper powder.
Avoid mango, litchi, custard apple, and grapes as evening fruit snacks on weeks where specified.
Avoid banana, pineapple, grapes, custard apple, chikoo, and lychee for mid-morning fruit snacks on weeks where specified.
If tulsi leaves are unavailable for the morning infused water, use cinnamon alone.
Ginger water can also be taken on an empty stomach as a home remedy for constipation.

general_lifestyle_tip_repeated: I always say eat healthy and don't diet.

sample_recipe_video_references.note: The BASIC_HEALTHY_DIET_Guidelines document includes a list of Instagram reel/post links as visual references for many recipes. URLs are preserved here for completeness but are external third-party content, not part of the structured recipe data.
